unit.note: F0 verification: shared/tests 3732 (12 skipped) + shared/scripts/tools/tests 139 (8 skipped) + shared/scripts/tests 196; 5 edited plugins pass (project 59 / design 19 / plan 43 / build 84 / deploy 26); ruff@0.15.15 clean; leak-guard f0 ALLOW

integration.note: integration-tests/ (2 slow deselected) — additive shared tooling, unaffected

degraded.detail: classify_complexity flagged touches_auth + touches_rls from message keywords, not the diff. The diff has no auth/RLS code (a Python gate-policy lib + CLI + prompt/doc edits). Overridden; the authoritative diff-driven risk flag is touches_io_boundary (resolver reads run_config + parses gate_catalog.json).

surface_verification.justification: SS2 is a Python lib + CLI + config-schema + prompt/doc change (gate catalog, resolver, honoring blocks in 5 phase skills). No web/UI/API surface, no server, no client code. run_config is only READ (mode); no message-contract change, so the Backend-affects-Frontend rule does not apply. The CLI was exercised end-to-end as empirical probes: --render-doc --output produced the committed pure-ASCII/LF docs/gate-catalog.md, and --gate/--phase resolved correctly under single_session/multi_session. Verification is the full F0 pytest suite (55 SS2 tests + 4067 shared/tools/scripts + 174 integration) + ruff + leak-guard f0 ALLOW.

behaviors.behavior: The catalog self-validates on load and raises GateCatalogError on any corruption (bad policy/phase/fires, auto-default missing a default_answer, a non-auto-default carrying one, a constitution gate marked auto-default, duplicate ids, covered_phases mismatch)
The resolver is inert under any non-single-session mode: every gate resolves to 'interactive' with should_stop=True (multi_session and unknown/typo modes)
Under single_session the resolver returns the catalog policy: auto-default proceeds (should_stop=False + default_answer), orchestrator-approve/hard-stop stop (should_stop=True, no default_answer)
Safety clamp: no constitution-locked gate ever resolves to a proceed (validator rejects it at load; the resolver fails closed even on an unvalidated catalog)
Defense-in-depth heuristic: any gate reading as a dangerous op (prod-/destructive/rollback/truncate/drop table/apply-fail) is constitution-locked, except a reviewed auto-recovery exception
Unknown gate id fails loudly for programmatic callers (lib raises KeyError) but SAFE for an LLM subagent (CLI resolves to interactive + stderr warning, exit 0)

Mode resolution precedence (--mode > SHIPWRIGHT_RUN_MODE env > run_config.mode > multi_session) and the run_config mode round-trip (Boundary Probe for touches_io_boundary), incl. missing/mode-less/corrupt config
Per-phase dry-run: every gate of each of the 5 phases honors the contract under single_session
Sensitive-gate policies match Sven's 2026-07-07 decisions: design mockups orchestrator-approve; the deploy/prod danger family + build destructive-sql/migration-apply-fail hard-stop + constitution
docs/gate-catalog.md is generated from the JSON, deterministic, pure ASCII, and byte-for-byte drift-guarded; the CLI --render-doc --output writer produces UTF-8/LF
CLI wiring: --gate resolution JSON, --phase --list, --render-doc(/--output), mutually-exclusive-action requirement, env/project-root mode sourcing
Each of the 5 phase skills honors the mechanism (a Single-Session Gate Discipline block invoking resolve_gate_policy.py for its phase) and the shared contract exists
behaviors.evidence: test_phase_gate_discipline_present.py (6 tests: 5 phases + shared contract) PASSED

behaviors.behavior: deploy/SKILL.md stays at its grandfathered 453-LOC bloat baseline (block added, offset by trimming a duplicate note); the 4 kernel skills stay <=300 LOC

enumeration_basis.note: Runtime honoring by a phase-runner subagent (observing no-END-TURN in a live phase) is introduced by SS3, not this diff; SS2 ships the mechanism + prompt contract + drift guards, all tested.
